title: Attention Optimizations
content:
# Interview Prep: Attention Optimizations

## Q1: How does Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) balance the trade-off between MHA accuracy and MQA inference speeds?

### Standard Answer
MQA achieves maximum throughput by reducing KV cache sizes to a single head, but it degrades model capacity and accuracy. GQA generalizes this by introducing a group parameter $G$. If $G=8$ and $H=64$, 8 query heads share a key-value head. This yields 8x memory savings over MHA while maintaining comparable generation quality.
